key_insight: RLVR instability can be explained by token-level gradient dynamics: whether updates raise or lower next-token probabilities and entropy depends on both the advantage sign and the current token distribution.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a simple way to make reasoning-model training more stable in practice by restricting policy updates to beneficial completions while preserving strong benchmark performance.
method: Derive a token-level taxonomy of gradient effects for GRPO-style RLVR updates.
Show that stability depends jointly on advantage sign and current policy token probabilities.
Introduce WAPO, an online clipped policy-gradient objective that updates only on positive-advantage completions.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports improved training stability and competitive or better results across mathematical reasoning and multi-hop QA benchmarks, spanning multiple model families.
watch_item: Because WAPO ignores negative-advantage completions, it may reduce learning signal diversity or require careful clipping/selection to avoid under-training.
paper_title: A Gradient Perspective on RLVR Stability and Winner Advantage Policy Optimization
